keyPoints: Races: When two threads try to access a shared resource, and the operating system decides which thread to run first.
Starvation: When a thread is unable to access a shared resource because other threads are holding onto it.
Livelocks: When two or more threads are unable to proceed because they are waiting for each other to release a resource.
Context Switching Overhead: When the operating system switches between threads, it incurs a performance penalty.
Priority Inversion: When a lower-priority thread is able to preempt a higher-priority thread, leading to unpredictable behavior.
